articles.title: OpenAI Launches GPT-5 with Extended Reasoning and Native Multimodal Generation
articles.summary: OpenAI officially released GPT-5, featuring significantly improved chain-of-thought reasoning, native image and audio generation, and a context window exceeding 1 million tokens. The model demonstrates near-expert-level performance on graduate-level STEM benchmarks and introduces a new "deep research" mode that autonomously plans and executes multi-step investigations. Enterprise API pricing reflects a shift toward outcome-based billing rather than per-token costs.

articles.title: Google DeepMind's Gemini 2.5 Pro Tops Coding and Math Benchmarks, Challenging OpenAI's Lead
articles.summary: Google DeepMind released Gemini 2.5 Pro with state-of-the-art performance on SWE-Bench, GPQA, and competitive programming tasks, narrowing or closing the gap with GPT-5 on several key metrics. The model introduces native tool-use grounding that allows it to call Google Search, execute code, and query structured databases mid-generation. Developers report particular strength in agentic workflows where the model must autonomously decide when and how to use external tools.

articles.title: Figure Robotics Deploys Humanoid Workers at BMW Spartanburg Plant at Scale
articles.summary: Figure AI announced that over 200 of its Figure 02 humanoid robots are now operating across multiple assembly lines at BMW's Spartanburg, South Carolina facility, marking the largest commercial deployment of general-purpose humanoid robots to date. The robots handle bin-picking, part insertion, and inter-station logistics using onboard vision-language models for task understanding. BMW reported a measurable reduction in line downtime and plans to expand the deployment to 500 units by Q3 2026.

articles.title: Boston Dynamics Atlas Now Runs Full Warehouse Shifts with Autonomous Task Switching
articles.summary: Boston Dynamics demonstrated its electric Atlas humanoid completing 8-hour warehouse shifts at a DHL facility, autonomously switching between palletizing, depalletizing, and package sorting without human intervention. A new reinforcement-learning-based locomotion controller allows the robot to handle uneven surfaces and recover from unexpected collisions gracefully. The company says commercial leasing will begin in late 2026 with pricing competitive against third-shift manual labor costs.

articles.title: Anthropic Introduces Claude Tool-Use Agents with Built-In Safety Monitoring Framework
articles.summary: Anthropic released a new agentic framework for Claude that enables persistent, multi-session tool-use agents with a built-in Constitutional AI safety monitor that audits every action before execution. The system supports sandboxed code execution, file system access, and web browsing while maintaining detailed audit logs for enterprise compliance. Early adopters in financial services report using the framework for automated due diligence and regulatory document analysis with human-in-the-loop approval gates.

articles.title: TSMC Begins Volume Production of 2nm GAA Chips, Powering Next-Gen AI Accelerators
articles.summary: TSMC confirmed volume production of its N2 process node using gate-all-around (GAA) transistor architecture at its Kaohsiung fab, with Apple and NVIDIA as lead customers. The node delivers approximately 25% better performance at the same power or 30% power reduction at the same performance compared to N3E. NVIDIA's next-generation Blackwell Ultra AI accelerators are expected to be among the first HPC products manufactured on N2, with shipments anticipated in late 2026.

articles.title: Neuralink Receives FDA Clearance for Second-Generation Brain-Computer Interface with 4x Electrode Density
articles.summary: Neuralink announced FDA clearance for its N2 implant, which quadruples the electrode count of the original N1 device to 4,096 channels and introduces bidirectional stimulation capability. The device is approved for expanded clinical trials targeting both paralysis patients and treatment-resistant depression. Three patients implanted during earlier trials have demonstrated the ability to control robotic arms and type via neural signals with latencies under 50 milliseconds.

articles.title: EU AI Act Enforcement Begins: First Compliance Audits Target Foundation Model Providers
articles.summary: The European Union's AI Office commenced its first formal compliance audits under the AI Act, focusing on general-purpose AI model providers including OpenAI, Google, and Meta operating in the EU market. Companies must now provide detailed technical documentation on training data, safety evaluations, and energy consumption for models classified as posing systemic risk. The audits are being closely watched as a bellwether for global AI regulation, with several non-EU jurisdictions signaling intent to adopt comparable frameworks.
